items.title: Buffett's Departure From Berkshire Hathaway Puts Spotlight on Greg Abel
items.description: Warren Buffett has retired as the CEO of Berkshire Hathaway Inc., marking the end of his six-decade-long tenure. Greg Abel, his successor, took charge on the first day of the new year, stepping into a role fraught with challenges. What Happened: Abel’s key responsibility will be the effective allocation of Berkshire’s burgeoning cash reserves, which recently crossed the $350 billion mark. items.title: 80-year-old Home Depot rival shuts down location, no bankruptcy
items.description: The home improvement and hardware retail sectors have been dominated by Home Depot and Lowe's for decades, shoving hardware cooperatives True Value Hardware and Ace Hardware out of the way and brushing off independent hardware retailers.
